books.title: Bleak House
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: A young woman of uncertain parentage is taken in by a kindly guardian, while her fate and that of two other young people hinge on the outcome of an interminable legal case.

books.title: Kim
books.author: Rudyard Kipling
books.summary: An orphaned street-urchin follows a holy man across India during the time of the British Raj, eventually gaining an education and becoming a recruit to the Great Game of espionage against the Russians.

books.title: Father Henson’s Story of His Own Life
books.author: Josiah Henson
books.summary: Josiah Henson’s autobiography recounts his life in slavery and his escape to freedom in Canada.

books.title: A General History of the Pirates
books.author: Captain Charles Johnson
books.summary: The lives and stories of the daring pirate outlaws who terrorized the Atlantic Ocean and Caribbean Sea in the 18th century.

books.title: Pollyanna
books.author: Eleanor H. Porter
books.summary: An orphan’s unrelenting optimism sees her through adversity and lifts up her new community.

books.title: You Never Can Tell
books.author: George Bernard Shaw
books.summary: A family recently returned to England navigates a comedy of errors when they accidentally meet their estranged husband and unknown father.

books.title: The Two Noble Kinsmen
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: Two cousins fall in love with a beautiful princess.

books.title: The Wood Beyond the World
books.author: William Morris
books.summary: An adventurer comes to an enchanted castle, where he must rescue a fair maiden from the clutches of an enchantress.

books.title: Michael Strogoff
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: A courier of the Russian Czar must race against time and the Siberian elements to deliver a crucial message of an impeding Tartar invasion and possible treachery.

books.title: The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: A London lawyer investigates the relationship between a brilliant scientist and a misshapen misanthrope.

books.title: The Bolshevik Myth
books.author: Alexander Berkman
books.summary: A left-wing radical records his experiences in revolutionary Russia.

books.title: The Phantom of the Opera
books.author: Gaston Leroux
books.summary: A mysterious presence terrorizes the Paris Opera.

books.title: Disenchantment
books.author: C. E. Montague
books.summary: A World War I veteran analyzes how the war affected the collective British psyche.

books.title: Essays
books.author: Errico Malatesta
books.summary: A collection of essays by Errico Malatesta.

books.title: Paradise Lost
books.author: John Milton
books.summary: A dramatic imagining in blank verse of the rebellion of Satan against God, Satan’s overthrow, and the Fall of Man.

books.title: Main Street
books.author: Sinclair Lewis
books.summary: An ambitious young woman chafes against the constraints of small-town America.

books.title: Plague of Pythons
books.author: Frederik Pohl
books.summary: A man navigates a world torn apart by bouts of spontaneous, unexplained possessions.

books.title: Call Mr. Fortune
books.author: H. C. Bailey
books.summary: A medically-qualified detective tackles mysteries ranging from a failed assassination to incidents at an all-girls school.

books.title: Mary Olivier: A Life
books.author: May Sinclair
books.summary: A gifted young woman born into a strict upper middle class Victorian family struggles for decades to balance duty to her controlling mother with a driving need to learn and to create her art.

books.title: The Innocents Abroad
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: Mark Twain travels abroad, offering witty observations on the places and cultures he encounters and commentary on the habits and foibles of his fellow travelers.
